decision: Widen the dynamic surface and skills; turn the admission cap into an elastic queue rather than raising it
rationale: User chose "widen surface+skills, cap as elastic queue": keep the 4GB/agent RAM ceiling exact, overflow queues instead of denying, widen what the model can express (loops/branches/recursion) and which skills it loads

decision: Full push: durability + observability included (all units 0-6)
rationale: User chose "full push incl. durability + observability" over a narrower slice

decision: Integrator hand-writes critical-path units; the fleet dogfoods the rest
rationale: User chose "I do critical-path units, fleet dogfoods the rest"; critical-path files (dispatcher/runner/admission/program-runtime/cli/hooks/phases/CI/pyproject/uv.lock) are integrator-only

decision: Ran the arena as 3 isolated candidates across three contested axes (where dynamic control lives / elastic-bound shape / event-log role)
rationale: Architect Phase B prescribes whole-shape diversity; CLAUDE.md + task arg forbid Opus subagents, so diversity came from forced-distinct biases on sonnet runners judged by the Opus main thread (documented deviation)

decision: Synthesis: base C1 (minimal seam); graft C3 closed-branch-keys + typed sealed events; graft C2 pure fold + journaled-verdict replay
rationale: C1 has the smallest blast radius and clean unit decomposition; C3's closed-key branch kills the unguarded-verdict failure mode; C2's fold + verdict-replay makes resume exact

decision: Reject C2 asyncio CentralScheduler and C3 SQLite store + first-class GraphSpec
rationale: asyncio is too much blast radius for a sync codebase; SQLite is a new NFS-unsafe dependency; the full graph is more than the predicate needs. Graph retained as the explicit Phase-E scrap-and-regrow fallback

decision: Foundational insight: the reserved=1 arithmetic does not move to one place, it stops existing
rationale: Because CapacityGate wraps the same AdmissionController the parent was admitted through, the parent's 4GB is already counted in in_flight_ram_gb; the shared RAM accounting already reserves the parent's slot

decision: Built committed verification harness measuring all four predicate dimensions; captured the pre-change baseline
rationale: figure-it-out: build the harness before the work so each later unit reads as a measured old-vs-new delta; reuse the proven real-FleetDispatcher load test (zero composer cost)

decision: Baseline: D1 width=5 (replan/branch/subprogram absent); D2 reserved=1 sites=3, CapacityGate absent, bounded load 0 denials vs negative-control 6 denials; D3 resume absent; D4 journal-query absent
rationale: The negative control reproducing 6 denials proves the harness can see the thing it measures; gates clean (ruff pass, ty 52 = baseline, 575 passed/4 skipped)

decision: Refine Unit 3 sketch: the gate edit lands in _execute_task (the real admit/deny site), not "the three producers call the gate"; name it AdmissionGate, not CapacityGate
rationale: Producers only size thread pools via effective_capacity; the actual try_admit-or-deny is dispatcher.py:558-570, where None becomes the admission.denied error. A blocking AdmissionGate.acquire() there turns deny into queue. FleetCapacityGate already exists in capacity/gate.py as a distinct cross-process watcher gate, so a second "CapacityGate" would collide

decision: One-way door identified: the parent task holds its admission token across nested child dispatch, so a blocking gate can deadlock when cap <= ancestor tokens
rationale: Token acquired at dispatcher.py:558 and released at 827; nested dispatch_dag/program runs in between holding it (the e2e "parent holds 1, production invariant"). Progress needs cap > ancestors: cap>=2 safe at one level, MAX_SUBPROGRAM_DEPTH < cap must be encoded for Unit 4 recursion. Settle the parent-slot-during-decomposition decision before the gate ships

decision: Resolve the door: keep the parent token held, nested children queue on AdmissionGate, encode cap > nesting depth (cap>=2 at one level); preserve today's deny for the degenerate cap<2 nesting case as a boolean can-nest guard
rationale: Matches the design doc claim that shared RAM accounting already reserves the parent's slot. cap>=2 guarantees >=1 free child slot so the queue drains; cap<2 keeps the existing deny path (a boolean, not a pool-size) so there is no permanent block and no test hang. The harness confirms it: at cap=3 today peak=2 (parent holds 1, children get cap-1), and the gate preserves peak=2 / 0 denied / 8 completed while dropping the 3 reserved sites. MAX_SUBPROGRAM_DEPTH < cap to be encoded with Unit 4

decision: Arbitrary-Python finding: the orchestrator ALREADY writes arbitrary Python to define workflows via the program runtime; the genuine gap is runtime-dynamic generation (Unit 4)
rationale: validate.py allows all Python control flow + the 5 primitives + safe builtins, banning only the escape surface (imports, open/eval/exec/getattr, async, with, yield, dunder) as a deliberate cooperative sandbox structurally identical to the Claude Code Workflow model. Static authoring exists; what is missing is an agent generating and running new validated workflow code mid-run (subprogram) and re-planning (replan). Reshape Unit 4 to deliver and surface that, keeping the sandbox boundary

decision: Design/de-risk workflow (10 sonnet agents, read-only) returned a synthesis: Unit3 GO_WITH_FIXES, Unit4 spec with ZERO AST relaxation, Units 2/5 journal+fold+resume spec, and surfaced a prerequisite planner bug. Integrator accepts the findings, overrides two of the Unit3 fixes (next row)
rationale: The fleet found two real Unit3 breaks (good, the door was de-risked) but its proposed fixes are themselves wrong; the integrator owns the concurrency fix, the fleet owns the finding

decision: FIXED the planner program-drop bug: plan() built TaskSpec without program=data.get('program'), silently dropping LLM-generated workflow source so the dispatcher program branch (gates on task_spec.program) was permanently dead. This is why arbitrary-Python-defines-workflow felt missing in practice
rationale: The whole program path shipped in v0.9.0 (1f4b7f8) and is wired end to end (schema allows program, from_dict populates it, prompt asks for it, dispatcher dispatches it) EXCEPT plan() dropped the kwarg. One-line mirror of from_dict + a regression test that feeds a program TaskSpec through plan()

decision: Integrator REJECTS the synthesis's two Unit3 fixes. (a) keep reserved=1 at runner.py:208 is a FALSE GREEN: it keeps the harness passing while leaving the gate blocking path untested (synthesis open-risk[1] admits fake_execute bypasses the gate via raw try_admit). (b) raise-when-held_count>=cap OVER-DENIES: it converts legitimate sibling queueing into denials at cap>=2, destroying the queue Unit3 exists to build
rationale: Correct fix: route the harness fake_execute through gate.acquire() so the real blocking path is measured, delete all three reserved=1 sites, keep ONE raw try_admit probe as the integrity check (still shows denials). Real deadlock condition is ancestors_held>=cap (a thread's lineage, not global held_count); thread an explicit nesting depth through dispatch and deny only when a thread's ancestors hold every slot

decision: IMPLEMENTED Unit3 AdmissionGate elastic bound. Added capacity_for + AdmissionDenied + a blocking AdmissionGate that queues overflow on a Condition and denies only when nesting depth >= capacity. Threaded explicit ancestor-aware depth through dispatcher._execute_task, primitives.run_one/run_many, dispatch_dag, dispatch_task_spec_children, and run_workflow_program. Deleted the reserved param from effective_capacity, taking the reserved=1 sites from 3 to 0
rationale: A held parent token plus children at depth+1 means siblings always occupy non-ancestor slots that will free, so a blocked child always eventually progresses; the only unschedulable case is depth >= capacity (ancestors already hold every slot), which is the sole deny. effective_capacity became a worker-count hint rather than the bound, so its reservation arithmetic was dead weight and was subtracted before the gate was added (subtract-before-you-add)
result: VERIFIED harness D2: reserved sites 0, AdmissionGate present, gate-routed wide rank completed=8/8 denied=0 peak=2 even with an oversized worker pool, raw try_admit probe denied=5 (visibility floor); 577 passed / 4 skipped, no hang; ruff clean; ty 44 (<= baseline 52, zero new diagnostics)

decision: IMPLEMENTED Unit4 dynamic-control surface: added branch (model-judged conditional), replan (model-triggered loop, MAX_REPLAN_ITERATIONS=3) and subprogram (runtime-generated nested workflow, MAX_SUBPROGRAM_DEPTH=3) as three new primitives in ProgramContext.build_namespace, with ZERO AST escape-surface relaxation. subprogram is the arbitrary-Python-defines-workflows capability the user asked for: a running program builds a new validated program string from agent findings and execs it sharing the same dispatcher/budget/deadline/result-list
rationale: Adding primitives needs only namespace injection plus widening the validators must-dispatch heuristic to count branch/replan/subprogram; _FORBIDDEN_NODES/_FORBIDDEN_CALLS are untouched so the cooperative sandbox boundary is unchanged. Recursion depth is a call-chain property (mirroring Unit3 admission depth) so it lives in thread-local state seeded across parallel/pipeline fan-out, not a shared counter that would over-deny legitimate parallel sub-programs; the agent budget stays the global resource ceiling
result: VERIFIED harness D1: primitives 5->8, new present [replan,branch,subprogram], none absent; 595 passed (+18) / 4 skipped, no hang; ruff clean; ty 44 (<= baseline 52, zero net new diagnostics after matching the fake dispatcher param name to _DispatcherLike); harness integrity OK

decision: IMPLEMENTED Unit2 durable typed run-event journal (fleet-built on sonnet, integrator-verified). New agent_fleet/orchestration/journal.py: RunEventKind StrEnum, frozen RunEvent with JSONL to_dict/from_dict round-trip, frozen AgentRecord, frozen RunState (completed_task_indices / pending_task_indices / agent_by_index), pure fold_journal + fold alias, load_journal, query_by_run, and a thread-safe fsync-durable append-only RunJournal. Scoped to two new files; no critical-path edits, no fleet commits
rationale: The journal is the keystone both remaining predicate dimensions need: D4 queryable observability is fold/query, D3 idempotent resume is RunState.completed_task_indices. Fold is a pure reduce with last-writer-wins terminal agent state keyed by seq, so the part resume depends on stays idempotent under replayed events; a private _AgentMutable folds into the frozen AgentRecord so illegal mid-fold state never escapes the module; from_dict parses on-disk data at the boundary. Non-critical-path, so the fleet built it while the integrator owns the run-path wiring in Unit5
result: VERIFIED harness D4: journal fold/query present [journal.fold, journal.RunState]; 618 passed (+23) / 4 skipped, no hang; ruff clean; ty 44 (<= baseline 52, zero net new diagnostics); scope respected (only 2 new files added, HEAD still a2fc9e0). Known Unit5 follow-up: seed RunJournal seq from the existing journal on resume so (run_id,seq) stays globally unique across restart, at which point log/phase fold becomes fully idempotent too

decision: IMPLEMENTED Unit5 idempotent crash-resume (integrator-owned, hand-written on the main thread because resume.py is tightly coupled to dispatch_dag internals). dispatch_dag gained journal+resume_state params: it folds the journal into a RunState, replays each completed task's reused result+output through the EXISTING skip-path machinery (_release + recursive _launch) without re-dispatching or re-journaling it, and emits run/agent lifecycle events for everything it does run. New resume_run reopens the same journal with its seq counter continued past on-disk events. Added an output field to AgentRecord so a reused task replays faithful downstream context (richer than summary).
rationale: Resume keys on the STABLE spec-position index (enumerate(spec.tasks)), never the volatile dispatch-completion index, so the finished-set survives a restart. seq continuation is load-bearing: a resumed agent_completed must out-rank the crash-run agent_failed under strict-> last-writer-wins, else the retried task stays failed forever and never converges (make-operations-idempotent). Skipped-due-to-upstream-failure tasks are NOT journaled so they correctly re-attempt; replayed-done tasks are never re-submitted to the pool nor re-journaled. Reused the skip cascade rather than a parallel done-path (laziness-protocol).
result: VERIFIED harness D3: resume entry point present [resume.resume_run]; ALL FOUR dimensions present, harness exit 0 = v0.11.0 stability predicate MET; 623 passed (+5) / 4 skipped, no hang; ruff clean; ty 44 (<= baseline 52, zero net new diagnostics); scope respected (2 critical-path edits journal+runner, 2 new files, no fleet-prohibited files, no fleet commits). Closes the Unit2 seq-continuation follow-up via RunJournal start_seq + test_resume_seq_continues_so_completed_beats_stale_failed.

decision: WIRED crash-resume into the operator CLI and ran the Phase E whole-product check. dag run gained --journal/--run-id (writes a durable resumable journal, prints the run-id); new dag resume subcommand folds that journal and re-dispatches only unfinished tasks. Factored the duplicated dispatcher/parent/defaults build into _build_dispatch_context and the ASCII/JSON tail into _emit_summary, shared by both handlers.
rationale: Structural harness presence (resume_run importable) is not the same as the capability being usable: the CLI wrote no journal, so an operator had nothing to resume from. Wiring the journal flag + resume subcommand is what converts D3 from present to works-well (experience-first). Shared helpers keep the two handlers from drifting (minimize-reader-load); the change subtracts duplication rather than adding a parallel resume code path (laziness-protocol).
result: VERIFIED on the real product: a CLI integration test drives cmd_dag_run --journal (fails one node, journals a,b completed) then cmd_dag_resume, asserting only the pending {c,d} re-dispatch and the journal folds to all-complete; plus a missing-journal error path. Whole-suite gate: 625 passed (+7) / 4 skipped, no hang; ruff clean; ty 44 (= baseline, zero net new diagnostics); harness exit 0 with all four of D1/D2/D3/D4 present. v0.11.0 stability predicate MET end to end.

decision: IMPLEMENTED Unit1 per-task skill selection + vendored agent-skills catalog (integrator-owned typed contract spine; fleet dogfooded the additive surface on sonnet via workflow wf_cfbd8a33-630). Spine: DagTask.skills tuple field + dag.schema.json + parse/to_dict mirroring allowed_paths; FleetTask.skills; fleet_task_from_dag_node threads skills=task.skills; resolve_dispatch_equip merges task.skills into extra_execute (guarded by skill_exists_in_base_kit, deduped vs loadout). Fleet vendored 23 addyosmani/agent-skills (MIT, LICENSE+ATTRIBUTION preserved) into base-kit/agent-skills/ and wrote the tests.
rationale: Delivers the design-doc Unit1 with NO new substrate: per-task skills ride the EXISTING extra_execute/compose_body path that systematic-debugging and pr-loop skills already use, so a selected skill flows into both skill_slots_execute and the prompt body unchanged (laziness-protocol, redesign-from-first-principles). Scoped to the DAG authoring surface (DagTask.skills) rather than adding TaskSpec.skills/planner emission with no consumer, since no model->DAG step exists yet (subtract-before-you-add). Integrator hand-wrote the typed spine because it crosses prohibited hooks.py + critical dag/runner.py/equip.py and the field/threading/merge are one coupled contract; fleet got the genuinely-independent new-file work.
evidence: agent_fleet/orchestration/dag/schema.py, agent_fleet/schemas/dag.schema.json, agent_fleet/hooks.py, agent_fleet/orchestration/dag/runner.py, agent_fleet/orchestration/equip.py, agent_fleet/base-kit/agent-skills/ (23 skills + LICENSE + ATTRIBUTION), tests/test_task_skill_selection.py, workflow wf_cfbd8a33-630
result: VERIFIED end-to-end on the real product: JSON DagTask.skills -> dag_spec_from_dict -> fleet_task_from_dag_node -> FleetTask.skills -> resolve_dispatch_equip lands the skill id in skill_slots_execute AND inlines its SKILL.md content (Measure before optimizing) into compose_body; non-existent skill ids silently dropped by the base-kit guard. Added a regression test for the fleet_task_from_dag_node threading link (the one spine edge the fleet test bypassed). 635 passed (+10) / 4 skipped; ruff clean; ty 44 (= baseline); harness exit 0, D1 still 8/8 primitives present.

decision: IMPLEMENTED Unit6 adversarial self-review gate (fleet-built loadout config + tests on sonnet; integrator fixed the one cross-cutting regression). Added agent-skills/doubt-driven-development + agent-skills/code-review-and-quality to the code_review review slots of the coder, pr-analyzer, and reviewer loadouts. The existing review phase (_review_skill_prompt_append -> _review_skills_from_slots reading task.equip.skill_slots_review) now injects an adversarial doubt-driven self-review into every code_review pipeline run.
rationale: No new pipeline substrate: the design-doc gate is realized purely by equipping the two vendored skills into the EXISTING review-slot injection path (laziness-protocol). The two skills are exactly the ones the design doc named. reviewer keeps its prior unslop+deslop review skills (additive, not replaced). The integrator owned the test_loadouts.py fix because the loadout change was a cross-cutting consequence the fleet agent\047s narrow gate missed; updated the brittle exact-equality assertion to pin the unslop/deslop prefix as a subset plus the two new gate skills, so the test states intent without re-breaking on future additions (encode-lessons-in-structure).
result: VERIFIED: all three personas review-slots contain both adversarial skills; _review_skills_from_slots emits both vendored SKILL.md bodies into the review prompt (proves the phase would inject them); both skills resolve in base-kit. Caught + fixed a stale exact-match loadout test via the full-suite re-run (the fleet sub-gate passed but the whole suite did not; prove-it-works). 635 passed (+10 across U1+U6) / 4 skipped; ruff clean; ty 44; harness exit 0.
